tweets.full_text:
Microsoft started by telling thousands of employees to experiment with Claude Code.

It became popular fast. Then they canceled most of the licenses.

The cover story: cost. The real reason: push employees to GitHub Copilot instead.

Same dynamic as Slack → Teams during lockdown. AI cost is the excuse. Product strategy is the motive.

tweets.full_text:
The Truth About Claude Code:
Yes — installing the right official Anthropic plugin does completely change the experience.
It scans your project and smartly recommends: → MCP servers
→ Hooks
→ Subagents
→ Skills
→ Automations
Then walks you through configuring everything step-by-step.
Most people are still using plain vanilla Claude Code… which is why their workflow feels chaotic and limited.
The real power isn’t just in Claude Code itself — it’s in the full ecosystem of plugins and tools around it.
If you’re serious about using Claude for development, set this up. Game changer. 🔥

tweets.full_text:
there's a reason why some people profit from new AI releases on day 1 while you're still watching tutorials...

they don't know the tools better than you, they know the principles

a software engineer gets almost the same quality from Claude Code, Codex and OpenCode... because he understands what's happening under the hood

an artist switches from midjourney to gpt-images-2 and the output stays fire... because they trained their eye, not their muscle memory on one UI

this year is absolute chaos on X

new model drops, new SaaS trending, new MCP server "changing the game" EVERY SINGLE DAY

if you chased every shiny thing you'd have switched your entire workflow 5 or 6 times

and you'd be starting from zero each time

here are some of the core principles you need to master:

> how LLMs fail (hallucinations, context limits, token behavior)
> how to structure instructions models actually follow
> how to design agent systems and memory architecture
> how to get the best outputs for the cheapest price

learn them once & apply them everywhere

user.name: Machina
user.description: running ai-powered agencies | https://t.co/fMOmHWBgHG

tweets.full_text:
Claude can’t magically copy any UI with one click.
The truth: You need Claude Code (the desktop app) + an MCP server (like Playwright MCP). Once set up, you can feed it a URL and it will analyze the site, extract colors/fonts/layout, then rebuild it in code.
It’s genuinely impressive and a real productivity boost — but it requires proper setup, isn’t built into https://t.co/z6h8lRQaUN, and isn’t 100% perfect on complex sites.
Great tool. Just not quite the “paste URL and it’s done” magic the hype suggests.

tweets.full_text:
Agree. But that wall in terms of what pure architecture can do is already hit. The step change function expressed in % is already slowing. The AGI - like (but not really AGI) gains in Claude Code (a'la coding agents) for instance have less to do with the core model than the harness surrounding it. 

All of this doesn't make LLMs any less useful for practical utilititarian purposes but from an AI / LLM engineering PoV that step change function is slowing. Post Feynman (which is slated for 2028) it may end altogether as compute itself hits a wall at 0.5 nm. 

Of course, if one fills the world with 1T GPUs then yes sure there is always some % to be extracted from model architectures but even that is not really organic from a model architecture PoV.

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code feels completely different once you install this.

Anthropic quietly released an official plugin that transforms Claude Code from a simple coding assistant into a full AI dev environment.

It scans your entire project and automatically recommends:

→ MCP servers
→ hooks
→ subagents
→ skills
→ automations

Then helps you configure everything step-by-step.

Most people are still using Claude Code completely vanilla…

which is why their workflow feels messy.

The real magic is the ecosystem around it 🤯

tweets.full_text: "Relentlessly interview YOU" is the line that hit. I'm building a /spec-it skill for Claude Code that pressure-tests a build idea before it earns a project folder. I had the interview capped at 3 rounds. Your framing made me drop the counter and let it run as long as the mental model is still shifting. Adding a 3-layer mechanic this week: predicate coverage, echo-back paraphrase, then Claude states the failure mode and asks if I agree.

user.name: JD
user.description: Pittsburgh native

tweets.full_text:
A faceless cat channel made ~$9,000 in three months. 

No camera. No editing timeline. No skill.

Mac Mini. Claude Code. one weekend.

Stock footage and a voiceover. People like me gobble it up.

Everyone fights over short-form pet content. 

Nobody touched long-form animated. The niche is empty.

ChatGPT names the channel and validates the topic. 

Invideo clones your voice once and writes every video after.

You don't record. You don't edit. You hit generate.

The catch nobody says: it only works if you ship one a week for five months.

tweets.full_text:
【Day2】AIマーケ構築 完全攻略プログラム アウトプット

《オリジナルAIシステム完全構築CAMP》

❶現状のAI環境
・Claude Code（VS Code上）でCLAUDE.md＋カスタムスキル使用中
・UTAGE API / MCPを接続
・議事録生成、Web制作のワイヤーフレーム、Remotion動画編集をスキル化
・クライアント様は「環境構築＋ナレッジ構築」をセットで提供中

❷AI環境の改善点
・スキルファイル・MCP設定が増えてきたので、フォルダ構成と棚卸し必要
・複数PCの場合、片方のPCに .envファイルの欲しい部分がなく、APIキーが使えなかった（今日気づきました！）
・UTAGE-API / MCPを活用しきれていない
・「学んだことを48時間以内に加工してシェアする」アウトプットフローがまだ手動

❸明日からのアクションプラン
・既存スキル・MCPの棚卸しと、.envを含むフォルダ構成の再設計
・学び→加工→シェアの一連を回すスラッシュコマンドを1本作る
・クライアントさま向け「環境構築代行＋ナレッジ代行＋スキル作成」を体系化
・「0秒で今すぐ使える」状態でクライアントに渡せるテンプレ整備

やりきれていなかったことをしっかりやりきります！
こうして根底から棚卸しして構築できるのが非常に楽しいです✨

ありがとうございます！！

@osaruproducer
@starriver0513
@ishida_chatgpt
